entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Cultivated self-pollinating tetraploid oilseed and cash crop

entities.name: BIOTIC AND ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Environmental factors that adversely affect groundnut and lead to reduced yield

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced, which is reduced by biotic and abiotic stresses

entities.name: GENETIC BASE
entities.description: The range of genetic diversity within a crop, narrowed by domestication and replacement of landraces

entities.name: GROUNDNUT BREEDING
entities.description: The process of improving groundnut cultivars

entities.name: ARACHIS WILD RELATIVES
entities.description: Wild species of the genus Arachis used as a resource for genetic diversity

entities.name: PLOIDY LEVEL DIFFERENCES
entities.description: Differences in the number of sets of chromosomes between wild and cultivated species

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of enhancing crop traits using genetic resources

entities.name: PRE-BREEDING
entities.description: The development of intermediate genetic material to broaden the genetic base

entities.name: SYNTHETICS
entities.description: Intermediate genetic material developed during pre-breeding

entities.name: INTROGRESSION LINES
entities.description: Lines developed by crossing synthetics with cultigens to incorporate desirable alleles

entities.name: DESIRABLE ALLELES
entities.description: Specific versions of genes that provide beneficial traits

entities.name: CULTIGENS
entities.description: Cultivated varieties of a plant

entities.name: CROSS-INCOMPATIBLE GERMPLASM
entities.description: Genetic material that cannot be successfully crossed

entities.name: LINKAGE DRAG
entities.description: The unwanted transfer of genes linked to a desired gene during breeding

entities.name: GENOMIC RESOURCES
entities.description: Tools and data used for genomic analysis

entities.name: GENOMICS-ASSISTED PRE-BREEDING
entities.description: Pre-breeding utilizing genomic resources to overcome linkage drag

entities.name: EXOTIC GENES
entities.description: Genes from wild relatives used for genetic enhancement

entities.name: GENETIC ENHANCEMENT
entities.description: The process of improving the genetic makeup of a crop

entities.name: GENOME SEQUENCING
entities.description: The process of determining the complete DNA sequence of an organism's genome

entities.name: GENE-EDITING TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Precise tools used to modify the DNA of an organism

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to withstand stresses

relationships.description: serves as a resource for broadening
